title: Optimize objectives, not isolated facilities
rule: Start from player objective, constraints, current coverage, and gaps before recommending facilities.
planner_implications: Facility lists are outputs of reasoning, not the start of reasoning.

title: Block impossible builds
rule: Unsupported facility types and impossible body/build combinations must be blocked rather than merely soft-warned.
planner_implications: Invalid recommendations should never reach the user as options.

title: Never imply direct station economy selection
rule: Do not tell the user to set a colony-type station to a chosen economy.
planner_implications: Express economy shaping as an indirect projected outcome.

title: Prefer local strong links for targeted market shaping
rule: For must-have outcomes, prefer same-body strong-link support over remote weak-link spillover as the planner's default recommendation.
planner_implications: Weak-link-only plans should be labeled experimental unless corroborated.
testing_status: Partly supported, still requires broader live verification
unknowns: Exact threshold at which weak support becomes reliable

title: Treat visible arrows as incomplete routing evidence
rule: Do not infer full causal structure from visible Market Links arrow count alone; treat arrow-count interpretation as a warning-bearing heuristic, not a complete routing proof.
planner_implications: Require live link inspection and caution before demolition or architecture changes.
testing_status: Supported by the canonical reference-source pack, still awaiting direct routing verification
unknowns: Exact aggregation behavior between main surface and orbital ports

title: Distinguish current buildability from proposed-plan totals
rule: A negative projected construction-point total that includes unbuilt facilities is not enough to declare the next selected build invalid.
planner_implications: Separate `current` from `proposed full-plan` states in the UI and logic.

title: Preserve negative evidence and contradictions
rule: Raw contradiction and failed predictions must remain visible instead of being flattened into a single confident answer.
planner_implications: Confidence must fall when credible conflicting evidence exists.

title: Label every recommendation by confidence band
rule: Every recommendation must be labeled `Confirmed`, `Likely`, `Experimental`, or `Unknown`.
planner_implications: The planner cannot output unsupported certainty.

title: Never universalize a contextual strategy
rule: A locally good architecture must not be promoted into a universal rule without context.
planner_implications: State the objective, gap, and trade-off that made a strategy viable.

title: Suggest experiments when confidence is weak
rule: When confidence is insufficient, recommend a validation experiment or observation step instead of pretending certainty.
planner_implications: Live verification entries are first-class outputs.

title: Resolve main-port priority before reading link topology
rule: Do not interpret same-body or cross-body routing until the planner has resolved which port is the Main Port on each body and whether another port has converted into a supporting-facility role.
planner_implications: Port hierarchy is a prerequisite input, not a cosmetic detail.
testing_status: Strong routing recommendation, still merits live edge-case verification
unknowns: Multi-port edge cases and some converted-port weak-link behavior

title: Do not recommend irreversible changes from arrow count alone
rule: Visible Market Links arrow count is not enough evidence to justify demolition, replacement, or other irreversible architecture changes.
planner_implications: Require live Market Links inspection or a targeted validation step before recommending destructive changes.
testing_status: Strongly supported planner-safety recommendation from the canonical reference-source pack
unknowns: Exact aggregation behavior in every mixed surface-orbital routing pattern

title: Dependency trees constrain build sequencing alongside CP math
rule: A planner must satisfy explicit prerequisite chains as well as construction-point availability before declaring a build order viable.
planner_implications: CP-positive does not automatically mean buildable.
testing_status: Confirmed as planner-safe logic by canonical Mega Guide, DaftMav extracts, and dependency-flowchart corroboration
unknowns: A few branch-specific `large-settlement versus family-level prerequisite` details remain worth spot-checking live
